title: console.log()
subtitle: Show the value
goal: Learn how to display values in the JavaScript console.
explanation: console.log() prints a value to the console.
It is one of a programmer's most important tools for checking how a program works.
With console.log() you can see variable values, calculation results, or messages.
The console helps find bugs and understand what is happening inside the program.
While learning, almost every example is worth checking with console.log().

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: console.log() is like a flashlight. It shines a light on what is inside your program.

remember: The result appears in the console.
It is the main tool for checking code.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Some beginners expect console.log() to show information on the website page. In reality, the output goes only to the developer console.

quiz.question: Where does the output of console.log() appear?
